title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for an Indian lunch or dinner. Get cooking!

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani with rice and naan, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a classic Indian delight.

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian dish renowned for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender pieces of marinated chicken. This iconic curry offers a perfect balance of sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy flavors, making it a crowd-pleaser for any meal. It is a true testament to the luxurious and comforting nature of Indian cuisine.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the cubed chicken with plain yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or unsalted butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding the pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is browned and cooked through. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of ghee or butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, Kashmiri red chili powder, and garam masala. Cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the cooked sauce mixture to a blender. Add about 1/2 cup of water or chicken stock. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. If you prefer an extra silky texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the sauce. Stir well to coat the chicken. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer and cook for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the richness of the sauce.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and sugar or honey. Crush the Kasuri Methi between your palms and add it to the curry. Simmer for just 2-3 minutes more; do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti.

tips: For an extra smooth sauce, soak the cashews in warm water for at least 15 minutes before blending. This helps them break down completely and contributes to a velvety texture.

Kashmiri red chili powder is crucial for achieving the signature vibrant red color of Butter Chicken without adding excessive heat. Do not substitute with regular chili powder if you want a milder, colorful dish.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day as the flavors deepen. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 2 months.
article:
Unlocking the secrets to truly authentic Indian cuisine at home can be a rewarding journey, and there’s no better place to start than with a dish that has captivated palates across the globe: the world-renowned Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian lunch recipe is a symphony of flavors, featuring tender chicken enveloped in a rich, creamy, and subtly spiced tomato gravy. It's a dish that promises comfort, luxury, and an unforgettable culinary experience, making it a perfect centerpiece for any meal.

Often gracing menus in high-end restaurants and humble dhabas alike, Butter Chicken holds a special place in the hearts of food lovers. Its vibrant color, aromatic spices, and velvety texture make it an irresistible choice, whether you're new to Indian food or a seasoned enthusiast. In this comprehensive guide, we'll delve into everything you need to know to recreate this masterpiece in your own kitchen, from its fascinating origins to crucial cooking techniques and serving suggestions. Get ready to embark on a flavorful adventure that will transform your understanding of what a truly exceptional Indian lunch recipe can be.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken, known traditionally as Murgh Makhani, translates literally to "chicken with butter." Its origins trace back to the 1950s in Delhi, India, at the Moti Mahal restaurant. Legend has it that the chefs, looking for a way to use leftover tandoori chicken, tossed it in a rich tomato-based gravy made with butter and cream. The result was an instant sensation, and thus, Butter Chicken was born.

At its heart, Butter Chicken is a celebration of contrasting yet harmonious flavors. The dish features succulent pieces of marinated chicken, traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) but easily adapted for home cooking by grilling, pan-frying, or baking. This chicken is then submerged in a luxurious gravy that forms the soul of the dish. This gravy is characterized by its smooth, velvety texture, derived from a blend of puréed tomatoes, cashews, and a generous amount of butter and cream. Aromatic spices like garam masala, kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), ginger, and garlic contribute to its complex flavor profile, which balances a slight tanginess from the tomatoes with a subtle sweetness and a mild warmth. It's this perfect equilibrium that makes Murgh Makhani an enduring favorite among Indian lunch recipes worldwide.

## Ingredients You Need

Making authentic Butter Chicken from scratch requires a few key components, but the effort is well worth the incredible flavor payoff. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast), cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, or regular for heat)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   Salt to taste
*   1 tbsp lemon juice

For the Gravy:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter
*   1 tbsp oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped or puréed
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 medium ripe tomatoes, puréed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, then drained)
*   1/2 cup water or chicken stock
*   1/2 tsp red chili powder
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1 tbsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), lightly crushed
*   1-2 tbsp sugar (adjust to balance tanginess)
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus extra for garnish)
*   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create a show-stopping Butter Chicken that rivals any restaurant:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine all the marinade ingredients with the chicken pieces. Mix well, ensuring the chicken is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, to allow the flavors to penetrate and tenderize the chicken.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F) or a grill pan on medium-high heat. Skewer the marinated chicken pieces (if using skewers) or arrange them on a baking sheet. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or grill/pan-fry until the chicken is cooked through and has a slight char on the edges. Set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt the butter and oil over medium heat. Add the chopped or puréed onion and sauté until golden brown and translucent, about 8-10 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Build the Gravy Flavor:** Stir in the puréed tomatoes, soaked cashews, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes, or until the tomatoes have softened and the mixture thickens, and oil starts to separate from the sides.
5.  **Blend and Strain:** Remove the pot from heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the clean pot. This step removes any fibrous bits from the onions or tomatoes.
6.  **Finish the Gravy:** Return the pot with the strained gravy to medium-low heat. Add the water or chicken stock, garam masala, crushed kasuri methi, and sugar. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer.
7.  **Combine and Simmer:** Gently add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir to coat the chicken evenly. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the gravy.
8.  **Add Cream and Garnish:** Stir in the heavy cream. Do not boil vigorously after adding the cream to prevent it from splitting. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed. Garnish generously with a swirl of fresh cream and chopped fresh coriander leaves before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Charred Chicken:** If possible, try to get a slight char on your chicken. This smoky flavor, reminiscent of a tandoor, adds incredible depth to the final dish. A hot grill pan works wonders.
*   **Smooth Gravy is a Must:** Blending the gravy base until absolutely smooth is crucial. Straining it afterwards, though optional, takes it to the next level, ensuring a velvety, lump-free texture that’s characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani.
*   **Don't Skip Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves are non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken. Lightly toast them in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding to the gravy to release their full aroma.
*   **Balance the Flavors:** Taste your gravy before adding the chicken. Adjust the sugar to balance the tanginess of the tomatoes, and add more salt or chili powder as per your preference. The perfect Butter Chicken strikes a harmonious balance between sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice dishes, making it a truly satisfying Indian lunch recipe.

*   **Naan or Roti:** Warm, fluffy naan bread or whole wheat roti are perfect for scooping up every last drop of the luscious gravy. Garlic naan or butter naan elevate the experience even further.
*   **Basmati Rice:** A side of fragrant basmati rice, whether plain or lightly spiced jeera rice (cumin rice), beautifully complements the richness of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Raita and Salad:** A cooling cucumber raita (yogurt dip) or a simple kachumber salad (diced onion, tomato, cucumber) provides a refreshing contrast to the creamy main course.
*   **Pickled Onions:** A small side of thinly sliced pickled onions adds a tangy crunch that cuts through the richness.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken is undeniably delicious, you can easily adapt this Indian lunch recipe to suit different preferences or dietary needs:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower, potatoes, and green peas to create Paneer Makhani or Vegetable Makhani.
*   **Healthier Version:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute some of the cream with low-fat yogurt or cashew paste for a lighter gravy. Grill or bake the chicken without added oil.
*   **Spice Level Adjustment:** For more heat, increase the red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the gravy. For a milder version, reduce the chili powder.
*   **Nut-Free Option:** If you have nut allergies, you can omit the cashews. While the texture will be slightly different, you can achieve some creaminess by increasing the onion or using a small amount of coconut cream.
*   **Smoky Flavor Enhancement:** If you don't have a grill or tandoor, a "dhungar" (smoking) method can infuse a smoky flavor. Place a small, hot charcoal in a bowl in the gravy, drizzle a little ghee on it, and quickly cover for a few minutes.

Mastering Butter Chicken at home is an accomplishment that brings immense culinary satisfaction. This beloved Murgh Makhani is more than just an Indian lunch recipe; it’s a testament to the rich tapestry of Indian flavors, offering a comforting and luxurious meal experience every time. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to delight your senses with a homemade Butter Chicken that will surely become a staple in your kitchen.
